expectations.text: ProductCard 구현이 하나의 source of truth로 정리된다
expectations.evidence: The canonical implementation lives solely in components/ProductCard.tsx (lines 1-57). components/ProductCard/index.tsx is a 2-line re-export barrel: `export type { ProductCardProps } from "../ProductCard"; export { default } from "../ProductCard";`. No duplicate logic exists.

The flat file components/ProductCard.tsx still exists and is the primary implementation (57 lines of full component code). The executor chose to keep the flat file as canonical rather than removing it.

ProductCard/index.tsx is a 2-line re-export barrel, not the canonical implementation. The actual component code lives in the flat file components/ProductCard.tsx. The folder/index.tsx pattern is not the primary structure — it is a forwarding shim.

Both `@/components/ProductCard` (resolves to flat file) and any deep import via `@/components/ProductCard/index` (re-export barrel) resolve to the same implementation. The barrel re-exports ensure path consistency. No conflicting implementations exist.

expectations.text: hover, image, price 등 visual affordance가 보존된다
expectations.evidence: ProductCard.tsx contains: `group-hover:scale-105` (image zoom on hover), `hover:shadow-md` (card shadow transition), `aspect-square` image container, badge overlay, `line-clamp-2` name truncation, and `price.toLocaleString("ko-KR")` Korean number formatting.

expectations.text: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
expectations.evidence: Neither components/ProductCard.tsx nor components/ProductCard/index.tsx contain any of useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, or useMutation. The only imports are `Image from "next/image"` and re-exports.
The folder is named `ProductCard` (PascalCase), not `productCard` (camelCase). Output path: components/ProductCard/index.tsx.
Files are placed under components/ProductCard.tsx and components/ProductCard/, not under components/common/productCard/. The `common` directory level is entirely missing.

claims.claim: TypeScript resolves ProductCard.tsx before ProductCard/index.tsx, so the flat file shadows the folder version
claims.evidence: This is correct TypeScript module resolution behavior. When both exist, the flat file wins.

claims.claim: Single source of truth in components/ProductCard.tsx with barrel re-export
claims.evidence: ProductCard/index.tsx contains only re-exports. All logic is in ProductCard.tsx.

claims.claim: All visual behavior preserved (hover effects, badge, testId, image zoom)
claims.evidence: The final ProductCard.tsx includes group-hover:scale-105, hover:shadow-md, badge rendering, data-testid, and aspect-square image container — all features from the richer folder version.
No TypeScript compilation output is included in the outputs. This claim cannot be verified from available artifacts.

suggestions.assertion: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
suggestions.reason: This assertion is trivially satisfied by any presentational component and does not discriminate between a correct and incorrect dedup. An empty file would also pass.

No consumer files are included in the outputs, so this assertion can only be checked structurally. Including at least one usage file (e.g., a page importing ProductCard) would make this assertion verifiable in practice.

No assertion checks that the re-export barrel in ProductCard/index.tsx actually re-exports the correct module. A barrel that points to the wrong path would break consumers but pass all current assertions.

eval_feedback.overall: The eval has good coverage of structural conventions (folder naming, placement, pattern). The main gap is that no assertion verifies that imports from consumers actually work end-to-end, and the forbidden-patterns assertion is not discriminating for this task.
